hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); echo "$INPUT" | jq -e ".tool_input.file_path // null | type == \"string\" and length > 0" >/dev/null; FILE_TYPE_STATUS=$?; if [ $FILE_TYPE_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then echo "BLOCKED: tool_input.file_path missing, empty, not a string, or hook payload JSON unparseable." >&2; exit 2; fi; FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // empty"); echo "$INPUT" | jq -e "(.tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // null) | . == null or type == \"string\"" >/dev/null; TYPE_STATUS=$?; if [ $TYPE_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then echo "BLOCKED: tool_input.new_string/content present but not a string." >&2; exit 2; fi; CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // empty"); CONTENT_STATUS=$?; if [ $CONTENT_STATUS -ne 0 ]; then echo "BLOCKED: failed to parse hook payload JSON (content)." >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE "\.(java|py)$" && echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiF "open-api.bingx.com"; then echo "BLOCKED: Hardcoded BingX production hostname (open-api.bingx.com) in $FILE. The base URL must come from the BINGX_BASE_URL environment variable, defaulting to the VST demo host (open-api-vst.bingx.com) in code -- never hardcode the production host as a source-level default or fallback. Going live is a deploy-time env-var decision, not a code change; see CLAUDE.md Non-negotiable Rules and Live Entry Criteria." >&2; exit 2; fi'
